fixes.description: Removed PT4CL1 --[+1]--> Lignin_SG_Ratio direct edge. Exploratory: PT4CL1 already reaches the phenotype via the cascade PT4CL1 -> P_Coumaroyl_CoA -> ... -> Sinapyl_Alcohol -> Lignin_SG_Ratio, so the direct edge potentially double-counts. Removing reduces SG hub from 6 -> 5 activators, which should reduce geometric-mean dilution and possibly help borderline-threshold tests.
fixes.reason: Diagnostic re-exam after iter 1: most remaining algebraic failures sit within +/- 0.05 of the ratio threshold, suggesting hub dilution. Reducing the hub by one activator was the lowest-risk candidate.
fixes.biological_justification: Wang 2020 (DOI 10.1104/pp.19.01550) attributes 4CL1 KO's S/G effect to cascade-level flux redirection. The direct edge to the ratio node was a modeling shortcut. Removing it keeps PT4CL1 -> cascade -> SG intact.

results.reason: REVERTED. Best-method (ODE) dropped 87.1% -> 83.9% (-3.2%). T014 (4CL1 antisense KD) and T022 (MYB21 LoF) moved from correct to incorrect: cascade propagation alone was insufficient to move the ratio across the direction threshold once the direct edge was removed. The hub-dilution reduction hypothesis was partially correct (some borderline algebraic cases moved), but the cost of breaking previously-passing tests dominated. RWR also fell sharply (-9.6%). Per REFINEMENT §8, reverted to iter 1 snapshot.
results.lesson: Direct PT4CL1 -> SG edge is not redundant with the cascade: the multi-hop metabolic path (PT4CL1 -> P_Coumaroyl_CoA -> ... 7 intermediate steps... -> Sinapyl_Alcohol -> SG) attenuates signal too strongly under geometric-mean activation, so the direct edge is doing real work in the propagation math. Keep.
